repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
There is a lot of overlap between terminal and prompt buffer, but no
easy way to limit the number of lines kept above the prompt to prevent
performance and other issues. This is desirable for both example
use cases in current documentation, chat UI and repl/shell plugins.

Solution:
Use existing 'scrollback' option to limit prompt-buffer lines
as well.

AI-assisted: Yes

Signed-off-by: Tomas Slusny <slusnucky@gmail.com>

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
"submit" the prompt by hitting Enter. The 'scrollback' option can be used
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -175,7 +175,9 @@ Put this in `uppercase.vim` and run:  >bash
 
 Prompt buffers provide a "prompt" interface: they are like regular buffers,
 except only the last section of the buffer is editable, and the user can
-"submit" the prompt by hitting Enter. Useful for implementing:
+"submit" the prompt by hitting Enter. The |'scrollback'| option can be used

comment_data.body:

@@ -261,6 +261,8 @@ These existing features changed their behavior.
   • |nvim_create_autocmd()|

+• 'scrollback' is now also valid in |prompt-buffer| buffers to limit the

comment_data.body:
do we need `extmark_splice` after this? that is the usual pattern.

(insert rant about how often we have to copy-paste these 3 things in numerous places... :P )
do we need to test weird edge cases like e.g. if the prompt itself exceeds 'scrollback'? e.g. `scrollback=1` with a 2-line prompt or something
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1125,4 +1125,30 @@ describe('prompt buffer', function()
       api.nvim_buf_get_lines(buf, 0, -1, false)
     )
   end)
+
+  it('respects scrollback', function()
+    exec_lua(function()

comment_data.body: looks like if the op is not kExtmarkNOOP then mark_adjust_buf calls the extmark_splice from inside already so should not be needed (i mostly copied the adjust_buf from terminal.c)
but if i use kMarkAdjustTerm as well then the cursor is also handled properly so I can reduce the code under by a bit
Its always using lines between start and the prompt start mark to both determine constraints for the scrollback and to delete lines so i figured its not necessary
